title: Galium angustifolium subsp. onycense
what_description: Galium angustifolium subsp. onycense is an infraspecific taxon of Galium angustifolium, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It is a perennial herb with slender stems, typically 10-30 cm tall, bearing narrow leaves, 1-2 cm long, and small, white or pinkish flowers in axillary clusters. The flowers have four petals and are 3-4 mm in diameter, with the calyx 2-3 mm long. This subspecies is endemic to the island of Crete in Greece and is characterized by its unique distribution and habitat. Its stems and leaves are covered with fine hairs, giving them a distinctive appearance.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Galium angustifolium subsp. onycense are inflammation, which is reduced due to its anti-inflammatory properties, and skin irritation, alleviated by its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. Galium angustifolium subsp. onycense has also been used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs, and gastroenteritis, with its antiviral and antibacterial properties. Additionally, it is used to heal wounds, due to its ability to promote wound healing, and alleviate digestive issues, such as diarrhea, through its antiseptic properties. Furthermore, Galium angustifolium subsp. onycense has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and pain, with its antipyretic, anti-rheumatic, and analgesic effects. Its antifungal, antiviral, and antibacterial properties also make it effective in treating infections, sore throat, eczema, and skin conditions, as well as nausea.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Galium angustifolium subsp. onycense are largely attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The anti-inflammatory properties of this plant are thought to be mediated by saponins and other triterpenoid compounds, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties are attributed to the volatile oils and saponins present in the plant, which have been demonstrated to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities. Furthermore, the plant's analgesic properties are thought to be mediated by the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to exhibit pain-relieving activity. The plant's antifungal properties are primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been demonstrated to inhibit the growth of various fungal species.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Galium angustifolium subsp. onycense are gallic acid, flavonoids, tannins, phenolic acids, and quercetin which have been isolated and identified through various studies. Gallic acid has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, while flavonoids have been reported to exhibit strong antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Tannins, particularly in the form of condensed tannins, have been found to contribute to the plant's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have been identified as key components of the plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Quercetin, a flavonoid with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, has been found to contribute to the plant's overall therapeutic potential.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Galium angustifolium subsp. onycense are derived from its aerial parts, specifically the leaves and flowers, which are used to make tincture, a concentrated liquid extract, by macerating the plant material in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin. An infusion is prepared by steeping the dried or fresh plant material in hot water to release its bioactive compounds, which can be used as a medicinal tea. A salve is made by infusing the plant material in oil, such as olive or coconut oil, and then straining it to create a topical ointment. This salve can be used to treat skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. An ointment is prepared by mixing the infused oil with other ingredients like beeswax and essential oils to create a semi-solid topical preparation.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Galium angustifolium subsp. onycense improperly include developing skin irritation due to its allergenic properties and irritating compounds, which may also cause nausea and vomiting due to gastrointestinal irritation. These side effects are attributed to the plant's high concentration of organic compounds, including alkaloids and terpenoids. Furthermore, Galium angustifolium subsp. onycense contains anthraquinones, which are known to cause gastrointestinal distress and lead to nausea and vomiting. If ingested in large quantities, it may cause more severe gastrointestinal irritation, potentially resulting in vomiting. In addition, skin irritation may progress to more severe skin conditions if not treated promptly.
